methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
MaverickX is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Eric Herrera that has raised $19M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the Climate & Energy sector, including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $19M raised as a proxy for current value, applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next-stage, yielding $48M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A)—producing a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M to $200M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $40M, median of $99M, and high of $172M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation multiples and whether peer companies at Series A truly represent forward-looking comparable positions for MaverickX. The peer-median result assumes MaverickX can reach Series A valuations in line with current market comps; the stage-progression method assumes more conservative near-term appreciation. Key uncertainties include the timing to Series A funding and competitive positioning relative to the identified peer set.
